hints.Domus: 'Domus' is a noun meaning 'house' — it is the subject
hints.orationis: 'Orationis' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of prayer'

hints.to eat: 'To eat' is 'edere/esse' — here 'est' is from 'sum, esse' (to be)

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect of 'sum' would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the principal parts of 'edo' (to eat) — same infinitive but different verb
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'fio' (to become/be made)

hints.genitive: Genitive of 'domus' is 'domus' (same form), but here it is the subject — nominative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'domum' — this 'Domus' is the subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'domus' but context shows this is a declarative statement, not address

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'domus' has genitive in -us
hints.2nd declension: 'Domus' has some 2nd declension forms but is primarily 4th declension
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'domus' has genitive in -us

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'oratio' — 'orationis' with -is ending is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'orationis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'orationis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'orationis' ends in -is

steps.prompt: What is the grammatical function of the second 'domus'?
steps.reveal: domus orationis = a house of prayer (predicate nominative)

hints.genitive of possession: The second 'domus' is nominative, not genitive — 'orationis' is the genitive
hints.appositive: An appositive renames the subject in the same case, but this is a predicate completing 'est'
hints.direct object: 'Est' is a linking verb and does not take a direct object

hints.subjective genitive: A subjective genitive would mean 'prayer's house' with 'prayer' as agent — this is purpose/characteristic
hints.objective genitive: An objective genitive would mean the house receives prayer, but the sense is 'a house for prayer'
hints.partitive genitive: A partitive genitive indicates the whole — 'orationis' describes the house's purpose, not a part

translation: My house is a house of prayer
